blurb: The call to be like Christ is not a demand for heroic achievement but an invitation to divine gentleness: to let the same mind that emptied and humbled himself think in us until ambition loosens its grip. At the heart of this passage lies a paradox—glory flows downward, through self-forgetful love, until every knee is drawn into the circle of mercy. Let these lines re-tune the quiet negotiations of your day; the way up is still down, and the humblest moment may become the doorway through which heaven surprises the earth.

verses.text: If there be therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,
Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind.
Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.
Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.
